channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump administration ramps up rhetoric targeting the courts amid mounting legal setbacks
item.description: The new populist president railed against the judiciary as they blocked his aggressive moves to restructure his country’s government and economy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Angst pervades a pair of Republican town halls
item.description: In two congressional districts and vastly different political environments, two Republican congressmen were met with far different reactions at public meetings they held late last week.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Makiya Seminera And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rhode Island Democrat lawmaker arrested for DUI ordered to install car breathalyzer
item.description: Rhode Island Democratic lawmaker Enrique Sanchez, who once filed a bill to allow illegal immigrants to vote, is facing a charge of driving while intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts welcome their newly arrived replacements to the space station
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Just over a day after blasting off, a SpaceX crew capsule arrived at the International Space Station on Sunday, delivering the replacements for NASA’s two stuck astronauts. The four newcomers — representing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tornadoes, wildfires and blinding dust sweep across US as massive storm leaves at least 33 dead
item.description: Violent tornadoes and high winds have decimated homes, wiped out schools and toppled semitractor-trailers as a monster storm that killed at least 33 people ripped its way across the central and southern U.S. Some of the initial storm deaths were reported Friday night in Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Roberson, Rebecca Reynolds And Julie Walker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Education Department staff cuts could limit options for families of kids with disabilities
item.description: For parents of kids with disabilities, advocating for their child can be complicated, time-consuming — and expensive.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Indiana cop charged after forcing daughter, 6, to stand in freezing cold with threatening sign
item.description: An Indiana police officer allegedly forced his six-year-old daughter to stand outside in cold temperatures holding a sign threatening her brother as punishment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump invokes 18th century law to speed deportations, judge stalls it hours later
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge barred the Trump administration Saturday from carrying out deportations under a sweeping 18th century law that the president invoked hours earlier to speed removal of Venezuelan gang members from the United States. U.S. District Judge James E.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: At least 32 dead in massive US storm after new fatalities reported in Kansas and Mississippi
item.description: PIEDMONT, Mo. (AP) — Violent tornadoes ripped through parts of the U.S., wiping out schools and toppling semitractor-trailers in several states, part of a monster storm that has killed at least 32 people as more severe weather was expected late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JEFF ROBERSON, REBECCA REYNOLDS and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked the Alien Enemies Act for the first time since World War II, granting himself sweeping powers under a centuries-old law to deport people associated with a Venezuelan gang.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan And Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration brushed aside decades of precedent when it ordered Columbia University to oust the leadership of an academic department, a demand seen as a direct attack on academic freedom and a warning of what’s to come for other colleges facing federal scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, KTLA 5

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant with multiple DWI, theft convictions deported again: ICE
item.description: A Mexican woman with a long criminal history who was previously removed from the U.S. four times was deported again on Thursday, immigration authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters rally against Department of Education layoffs
item.description: Demonstrators gathered outside the U.S. Department of Education on Friday morning to protest mass firings and what some are calling an effort to dismantle the federal education system.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, KTLA 5

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma brought by the Biden administration’s Department of Justice, which challenged the states' immigration laws making it a crime for someone to be in the state if they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arrest made after crash kills at least 5 people, including infant, in Texas
item.description: At least five people died and 11 were hurt in a crash involving at least 17 vehicles, including semi-trucks, on Interstate 35 in north Austin late Thursday night, police said.
item.dc:creator: Valentina Misas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund was meant to allot $22.8 billion to cover expanded benefits for former service members sickened by military toxic exposures — including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange water contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Education Department investigating dozens of universities after DEI warning letter
item.description: The Education Department announced Friday it is investigating more than 50 colleges and universities over what it called "racial preferences" in academics or scholarships, a move that comes amid the Trump administration's wider crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: American draws Australian PM's ire after snatching wombat
item.description: Australian immigration officials could bar an American influencer from ever returning Down Under for carrying a baby wombat away from its mother.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin authorities release details on missing teen's recovery
item.description: Lillian Asala, a 13-year-old from Wisconsin, was reported missing on March 1. Her mother was arrested in connection to her disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, NewsNation

item.title: Former US Sen. Alan Simpson dies at age 93
item.description: Former U.S. Sen. Alan Simpson, a political legend whose quick wit bridged partisan gaps in the years before today’s political acrimony, has died. He was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Second judge blocks mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: A second federal judge has blocked the Trump administration from firing federal workers, issuing an order Thursday that probationary employees be reinstated weeks after their dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Newborn kittens rescued from wrecked car in New York moments before demolition
item.description: A litter of kittens was rescued from the inside of a wrecked car at a junkyard moments before it was scheduled to be demolished Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democratic Senate leader caves to Republicans in government shutdown standoff and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: John Feinstein, one of country's foremost sports writers, dies suddenly
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Columbine victim's death from health problems related to 1999 school shooting ruled a homicide
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The death of a woman who was partially paralyzed in the Columbine High School shooting has been ruled a homicide, raising the death toll of the 1999 attack to 14. Anne Marie Hochhalter died Feb.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WGN 9
